title: azalea pumila
intro: Azalea pumila, also known as the dwarf azalea, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of China and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Azalea pumila include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Azalea pumila often involve drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be made into teas, infusions, or used in traditional medicine formulas.
